summary: The task is complete in substance. GET /rest/tools is implemented as a scoped Next.js route, uses the existing Basic auth resolver with no-auth allowed and invalid/unsupported Authorization failing closed, returns enabled endpoint metadata in the documented REST shape, and does not implement out-of-scope REST calls or OAuth filtering. The task maps directly to docs/product-specs/rest-mock-api.md, related docs were updated, and recorded required checks all passed.
satisfied_exit_criteria: REST list response matches PRD metadata shape: tools are enabled-only, sorted by name, and include name, title, description, and parameter metadata with defaultValue when configured.
No-auth and valid Basic behavior are covered by @rest-tools-list E2E tests.
Invalid Basic returns the documented 401 response with WWW-Authenticate and unauthorized JSON body; unsupported Authorization also returns 401.
All required checks passed: npm run test:unit, npm run test:e2e -- --grep @rest-tools-list, and npm run verify.
